title: Cyathea × elliottii
intro: Cyathea × elliottii, also known as the Elliott's tree fern, is a hybrid species of tree fern native to Australia. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Cyathea × elliottii include polysaccharides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
taxonomy: Cyathea × elliottii is a hybrid species of ferns in the family Cyatheaceae. It is a cross between the species Cyathea brownii and Cyathea medullaris. This hybrid fern is native to Australia and New Zealand. Cyathea × elliottii is also known as the Queensland cycad fern or the Queensland tree fern. The classification of Cyathea × elliottii is a part of ongoing research in the field of botany.

This plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties. The fronds of the Cyathea × elliottii are used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever and skin conditions.
